jobs.title: Senior Hydrogeologist
jobs.description:
Overview

We are seeking a Senior Hydrogeologist to help us shape the future. In this role, you will have the opportunity to perform and lead environmental analyses in support of nuclear environmental licensing projects nationwide. This exciting and impactful role can be based in one of our offices located in:

Kennesaw, GA (Suburb of Atlanta)
Oklahoma City, OK
Naperville, IL
Overland Park, KS
Dallas, TX
Fort Worth, TX
Any ENERCON office will also be considered

Responsibilities

Imagine a day where you’re solving complex groundwater challenges — from modeling underground systems to leading field investigations — making a meaningful impact on the world around you.

Groundwater Modeling: Develop, calibrate, and analyze groundwater flow models using MODFLOW to assess and predict hydrogeologic conditions
Field Investigations & Data Collection: Develop and conduct field investigations to characterize hydrogeologic systems and evaluate groundwater flow conditions. Collect and evaluate site-specific groundwater data to characterize groundwater flow patterns, evaluate the extent of possible groundwater impacts, and identify any possible receptors. Perform and interpret aquifer tests to measure hydraulic properties. Evaluate site-specific groundwater data to characterize groundwater flow patterns and water quality
Impact Assessment & Remediation: Evaluate the potential impacts of development, land use changes on groundwater resources. Develop remediation plans for contaminated groundwater sites. Monitor remediation activities and assess their effectiveness over time
Regulatory Compliance & Permitting: Ensure compliance with local, state, and federal regulations related to groundwater management, water quality protection, and environmental permitting. Help prepare permit applications and environmental impact assessments as required
Reporting & Communication: Prepare comprehensive technical reports and presentations summarizing findings from hydrogeological studies and investigations. Communicate complex scientific concepts to clients and regulatory agencies in a clear and understandable manner
Project Management: Manage hydrogeological projects from start to completion, including budgeting, scheduling, and coordination of fieldwork and laboratory testing activities
Other areas of focus include: NEPA Compliance, Geographic Information System (GIS), Natural Resources Assessments & Permitting, Geoscience & Remediation Services, Radiological Services and Regulatory Compliance

Qualifications

Bachelor's Degree in Hydrogeology or Geology
Must have a minimum of 6 years of hydrogeology experience
Proficiency in hydrogeologic principles to include characterization, assessments and monitoring
Experience with field instrumentation and techniques for groundwater sampling and monitoring
Knowledge of environmental regulations and permitting processes related to groundwater
Excellent writing, communication, strategic thinking, and “people” skills
Ability to travel between 10%-20% of the time; duration of travel may vary depending on client needs, ranging from a few days to a week.

Compensation Range

USD $82,000.00 - USD $145,000.00 /Yr.

Regulatory Compliance & Permitting: Ensure compliance with local, state, and federal regulations related to groundwater management, water quality protection, and environmental permitting. Help prepare permit applications and environmental impact assessments as required
Reporting & Communication: Prepare comprehensive technical reports and presentations summarizing findings from hydrogeological studies and investigations. Communicate complex scientific concepts to clients and regulatory agencies in a clear and understandable manner
Project Management: Manage hydrogeological projects from start to completion, including budgeting, scheduling, and coordination of fieldwork and laboratory testing activities

jobs.title: Technical Solutions Engineer II
jobs.description:
The Technical Solutions Engineer II serves as a technical expert and problem solver, supporting customers and internal teams by applying chemical and engineering knowledge to real-world applications. This role acts as the engineering bridge between product development, manufacturing, and customer needs by providing technical guidance, troubleshooting support, and application expertise.

This position is responsible for understanding field applications and translating them into practical, cost-effective engineered solutions. The Technical Solutions Engineer II designs application simulations and solution pathways providing data-driven engineering recommendations that ensure product performance and successful application outcomes.

Position Responsibilities

Technical Support & Customer Solutions

Provide technical expertise to customers regarding product selection, application, performance, and optimization.
Troubleshoot customer issues using structured analysis, laboratory testing, plant trials, and on-site evaluation when required.
Translate customer requirements into practical, data-supported engineering recommendations.
Identify when existing products can be adapted versus when new solutions are required.
Support Sales with technical presentations, product demonstrations, and customer-facing discussions.
Escalate systemic product or application issues appropriately to R&D or Manufacturing.
Engage directly with customers when needed to clarify technical intent and constraints.

Application Testing & Simulation

Design and operate test stands that simulate end-user application environments.
Move beyond benchtop testing to controlled, real-world performance validation.
Establish repeatable testing protocols, structured documentation, and application knowledge retention practices.
Generate defensible data to support internal and external technical recommendations.

Product & Process Engineering Support

Partner with R&D on formulation adjustments, product modifications, and new product introductions to ensure application feasibility, manufacturability, and alignment with customer performance requirements.
Provide engineering support to Manufacturing for process optimization, process improvement, and issue resolution.
Identify opportunities to improve product performance, manufacturability, scalability, and cost efficiency with plant operations.
Evaluate engineering solutions relative to plant capabilities and collaborate with Manufacturing and Quality to validate production feasibility.

Supplier & Contract Manufacturing Evaluation

Evaluate internal production capability relative to customer requirements and identify external manufacturing options when necessary.
Conduct technical capability assessments of external manufacturing partners when internal capabilities do not align with customer requirements.
Support supplier qualification processes in partnership with Manufacturing and leadership.
Develop technical documentation required for external production execution.
Support technical transfer and validation activities when external production pathways are implemented.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Lead technical initiatives across functions without direct authority.
Drive clarity in technical decision-making and structured experimentation.
Escalate risks and tradeoffs appropriately.
Contribute to the long-term development of Cortec’s technical solutions capability.
Identify recurring technical issues and trends to support continuous improvement initiatives.

Other

Other related duties as assigned.

JOB SPECIFICATIONS

Minimum Education
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Materials, or related field required). Minimum Experience
Minimum 5 years of engineering experience in manufacturing or industrial environments.

Experience Translating Customer Technical Requirements Into Engineered Solutions.

Experience working cross-functionally with R&D and Manufacturing.

Proven ability to succeed in customer-facing technical roles, building trust and delivering technical solutions that meet customer needs.

Ability and willingness to travel approximately 25% as required to support customer and plant initiatives.

new_jobs.title: Technical Solutions Engineer II
new_jobs.description:
The Technical Solutions Engineer II serves as a technical expert and problem solver, supporting customers and internal teams by applying chemical and engineering knowledge to real-world applications. This role acts as the engineering bridge between product development, manufacturing, and customer needs by providing technical guidance, troubleshooting support, and application expertise.

This position is responsible for understanding field applications and translating them into practical, cost-effective engineered solutions. The Technical Solutions Engineer II designs application simulations and solution pathways providing data-driven engineering recommendations that ensure product performance and successful application outcomes.

Position Responsibilities

Technical Support & Customer Solutions

Provide technical expertise to customers regarding product selection, application, performance, and optimization.
Troubleshoot customer issues using structured analysis, laboratory testing, plant trials, and on-site evaluation when required.
Translate customer requirements into practical, data-supported engineering recommendations.
Identify when existing products can be adapted versus when new solutions are required.
Support Sales with technical presentations, product demonstrations, and customer-facing discussions.
Escalate systemic product or application issues appropriately to R&D or Manufacturing.
Engage directly with customers when needed to clarify technical intent and constraints.

Application Testing & Simulation

Design and operate test stands that simulate end-user application environments.
Move beyond benchtop testing to controlled, real-world performance validation.
Establish repeatable testing protocols, structured documentation, and application knowledge retention practices.
Generate defensible data to support internal and external technical recommendations.

Product & Process Engineering Support

Partner with R&D on formulation adjustments, product modifications, and new product introductions to ensure application feasibility, manufacturability, and alignment with customer performance requirements.
Provide engineering support to Manufacturing for process optimization, process improvement, and issue resolution.
Identify opportunities to improve product performance, manufacturability, scalability, and cost efficiency with plant operations.
Evaluate engineering solutions relative to plant capabilities and collaborate with Manufacturing and Quality to validate production feasibility.

Supplier & Contract Manufacturing Evaluation

Evaluate internal production capability relative to customer requirements and identify external manufacturing options when necessary.
Conduct technical capability assessments of external manufacturing partners when internal capabilities do not align with customer requirements.
Support supplier qualification processes in partnership with Manufacturing and leadership.
Develop technical documentation required for external production execution.
Support technical transfer and validation activities when external production pathways are implemented.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Lead technical initiatives across functions without direct authority.
Drive clarity in technical decision-making and structured experimentation.
Escalate risks and tradeoffs appropriately.
Contribute to the long-term development of Cortec’s technical solutions capability.
Identify recurring technical issues and trends to support continuous improvement initiatives.

Other

Other related duties as assigned.

JOB SPECIFICATIONS

Minimum Education
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Materials, or related field required). Minimum Experience
Minimum 5 years of engineering experience in manufacturing or industrial environments.

Experience Translating Customer Technical Requirements Into Engineered Solutions.

Experience working cross-functionally with R&D and Manufacturing.

Proven ability to succeed in customer-facing technical roles, building trust and delivering technical solutions that meet customer needs.

Ability and willingness to travel approximately 25% as required to support customer and plant initiatives.
